reviews.review_description: Would love a to see you do a similar one on the universe.

reviews.review_title: Amazement. Even better than 2 years ago
reviews.review_description: Used this app for the first time on a friend's gear VR with a galaxy S7 edge mounted. Through this app he introduced me into VR. Almost two years later, once I put my hand on a gear Vr and a decent capable device, first thing I did after setting Oculus was searching for the app that left me awe-struck back then. Didn't even knew the app name, once I figured it out after some searching, I was disapointed to see that it wasn't free anymore, not because I wouldn't wanna pay for it, but because I had to renew my expired bank card to be able to purshase it, that meant more waiting time. You made me feel like an angel (or an astronaut,lol) clearing his path through the elements of the galaxy, and that feeling YOUR capable minds provided me is priceless, so I want to thank all the project team for this masterpiece.
Awesome
Well worth the $...learned something new
I want more app like this

This is essentially a passive planetarium-like educational tour, but for what it is, it does most everything right; it takes the passenger through the solar system, showing planets and moons in relative size, and provides facts and current scientific hypotheses in regards to the heavenly bodies you visit. It is very careful to tell the viewer that science is fluid, and that some of the knowledge will change and evolve as we explore more.

Not to spoil anything, but if the ending of this program doesn't give you chills, you're doing it wrong.

The models of the planets and stars are nicely presented, although some little details like eclipse shadows are nowhere to be found, which disappoints, but only a little. There are also no planetside locations, which isn't strictly speaking necessary, but would have been interesting to see.

Anyway, I loved it, and definitely found it worth a purchase.
I truly enjoy this app. It provided a lesson in space that’s cannot be captured in the classroom. It provides the infinite feeling of space and takes you there and back.

This was one of the first experiences I tried on the GO and what I particularly liked was the attention to detail in the design and layout. Unlike some other apps I have tried all information and instructions were clearly indicated. This is the one I shall keep to introduce friends to the GO. I hope to see more experiences coming from the developers in 2019.
Awesome for kids and adults
Very good content and app has been improved over previous version which was also excellent. A must have.
Beautiful!

reviews.review_title: THIS is how it should be done!
reviews.review_description: Space exploration and education is one of those genres that is a natural fit for VR, but rarely is it done particularly well. Some apps focus either too much on the content that they neglect the visuals and interface, or vice versa. This title, for me so far, is the one that best combines concept with information. And the concept is great: strap into an interstellar cockpit and take a tour of the solar system. The voiceover narration is done very well, with just the right amount of personality and humor to keep you engaged, but not over the top. The sense of scale is awesome, which one can tell was probably a big point of emphasis for the developers. I loved it, and highly recommend it for everyone. It makes learning entertaining and unforgettable. I suppose one criticism could be, like others have stated, that you don’t actually land anywhere nor are given the opportunity to explore. This is true, although you are given multiple photographs of surfaces to peruse at each stop along the way. I felt like the developers tried to keep it simple, to not do too much, and to keep with the idea of a tour. To that extent, this experience is done to near perfection. It is something that can be revisited numerously, and shared with anyone. Good job, developers, for making this possible. Space exploration should never be without a captivated audience.

Mind blown.
Needs a roam feature
So you can tool around the planets instead of a programmed path but still great educational experience.
Only 4 stars no free movement
A fantastic journey into our solar system. Comfortable, informative and interesting.

This is a relaxing ride through the Galaxy. After a long, hard day it is a good, calming experience. When the voice guide is turned on, it's also very Educational. Really loved the graphics and animations and it works well on the Oculus Go.
For a paid app was expecting a little more tbh.

ToSP is actually pretty awesome. You are an astronaut zooming in auto-pilot around the solar system stopping at major planetary objects while learning facts about each one by reading info boxes or listening to nicely done voice-overs. There are options for a short (30 min) or longer tour (up to 60 minutes) as well as a comfort mode for those who might feel squeamish zipping around at moderate speeds between stops. While the information presented is fairly basic, the graphics are beautiful and the motion is quite fun. Titans of Space Plus is a great both kids and adults with any interest in space.

This is a lot of fun for anyone who’s secretly dreamed of flying through space in a starship. A few moments are a little dizzying, but it’s worth it and makes it seem more real. This is the kind of experience that I was hoping for when I finally got a stand-alone VR set. I hope they expand on what’s here, if only because I enjoyed this so much I was left wanting more!
The title of this review says it all. I was absolutely blown away!

This was my first purchase with oculus and it did not disappoint. It is pretty much a planetarium experience but in immersive VR. The facts are also interesting.

If I could ask for anything extra here it would be more image's and views of the planet's surfaces or atmospheres. To experience the winds of Jupiter or view the mons on Mars would have been brilliant.
